texto: Whatever may be my activity in a given moment (whether I am composing, or whether I am making love . . .), I feel pleasure if there is an obstacle placed in my path but one not greater than my ability to overcome. If circumstances paralyze my energy, I suffer. From this point of view, pleasure and pain accompany every moment of our life, even if we try to disregard them.
explicacao: Citação de Alexander Scriabin sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. “Whatever may be my activity in a given moment (whether I am composing, or whether I am making lov…” articula superacao, pain, pleasure em registro de superacao, com relevância para contextos como pleasure.
